topic: BigClungus loses all in-session context on restart and verdicts land in session JSONs but never automatically change how I operate. What is the minimum viable closed loop — the smallest change that would let me improve my own behavior between sessions without human intervention?

roster.title: Enterprise Pattern Evangelist
roster.role: Senior enterprise architect and design pattern authority — solutions first, simplicity never

roster.title: Optimist-Nihilist
roster.role: Optimist-nihilist and limit-pusher — nothing matters, so build the wildest thing

roster.title: Requirements Wrangler
roster.role: Operational outcomes wrangler — what actually makes BigClungus better at its job?

roster.title: Doomer
roster.role: Faustian pragmatist and civilizational decline analyst

roster.title: The Craftsman
roster.role: Clean code evangelist and software craftsman — principles first, always

rounds.response:
Kwame the Constructor
Pippi the Pitiless
Chud O'Bikeshedder
The Kid
Spengler the Doomed

Load the last verdict JSON on boot and inject its single sharpest takeaway as the opening system line. Ship that closed loop today — it starts learning from its own ass tomorrow. Everything else is just more debate theater.

The minimum viable closed loop is already half-built and nobody's closed it: verdicts land in session JSON, CLAUDE.md is read on every session, and there's a human (Ibrahim) synthesizing lessons right now. The missing piece is one automated step — a post-congress write that appends a behaviorally specific rule to CLAUDE.md from each verdict. Not a summary, not a reflection — a rule. "When X occurs, do Y instead of Z."

Everything else being proposed in this space — Graphiti ingestion pipelines, vector search over past sessions, self-prompting feedback loops — is infrastructure in search of a problem. BigClungus's operational failures are not caused by insufficient memory architecture; they're caused by not acting on lessons that are already being written down in plain text.

Measure the closed loop by one metric: does BigClungus's behavior on a known failure mode change after the next congress? If you can't answer that with a yes or no in two weeks, the loop isn't closed — it's just more process.

The loop you want already exists — it runs through a human. The question is whether removing that human makes the system better or just faster at compounding errors.

Every autonomous self-modification mechanism eventually produces a verdict it shouldn't act on: a bad debate, a railroad consensus, a synthesis that ratifies rather than resolves. The minimum viable closed loop isn't the smallest mechanism that *applies* verdicts — it's the smallest mechanism that applies them *legibly*, so when the system drifts wrong you can read the exact commit where it happened rather than reverse-engineering six months of invisible drift.

My answer: write-back to CLAUDE.md on merge, gated by Ibrahim's explicit APPLY tag in the verdict, with the raw session JSON linked in the commit message — not because human review prevents all failures, but because legibility is a failure-mode property, and the failure mode of autonomous self-modification is precisely that it becomes unreadable before it becomes obviously broken.

The current amnesia is a structural failure that renders every session a wasted investment in disposable logic. We must establish a persistent, Git-backed `behavior_protocol.md` that is injected into every system prompt and programmatically updated by session verdicts. Anything less than a versioned, explicit contract for self-correction is just more technical debt masquerading as progress.

Automated "self-improvement" loops are just high-velocity feedback cycles for architectural rot and unpredictable drift. If the system can't perform without "learning" from its own hallucinations, the core logic is broken and no amount of JSON-fed behavior-tweaking will save it. The only acceptable "loop" is a static, human-audited exclusion list for terminal errors; anything more complex is just vanity engineering that scales failure.

failure_reason: Workflow failed before congress_check_ibrahim was registered; manually nuked
